$description: Single source for generated Cache Cow design tokens (issue 005). Values are transcribed from DESIGN.md: section 3.1 core color tokens, section 3.2 verified contrast pairs, section 4 typography, section 5.2 cache-status vocabulary. DESIGN.md owns these values (CLAUDE.md: rules live in the owning canonical document) — change DESIGN.md first, then mirror here. Clients consume only the generated artifacts in tokens/dist/ (ARCHITECTURE.md, Dependency rule 8).

300.role: Brand surface: hero bands, cards, icon tile

500.role: Primary action, price highlights, sale, the smoke arcs

700.role: Action hover/pressed, ember text on light surfaces when 4.5:1 is required

display.usage: H1, hero, section titles, big prices on promo; one display moment per viewport (DESIGN.md 4.1)

body.usage: UI and body: body 400 at 17px/1.6, UI labels 500, subheads 700 (DESIGN.md 4.1)

data.usage: Prices, SKUs, order and invoice numbers, weights, dates in tables, API docs, cache-status badges (DESIGN.md 4.1)

latin.notes: de-DE: hyphens auto with correct lang attr; avoid all-caps nav labels in German (DESIGN.md 4.2)

ja-JP.notes: Display: weight 900 plus 6px Ember slab underline bar; no italics (DESIGN.md 4.2)

hi-IN.notes: Same slab-bar display treatment; line-height 1.7 minimum for matra clearance (DESIGN.md 4.2)

scale.notes: Modular scale 1.250 from 17px base; Plex Mono data sits one step below its surrounding Archivo text (DESIGN.md 4.3)

cacheHit.plainLine: Ships today from your regional cold store
cacheHit.notes: Badge is always paired with the plain-language line: status is never conveyed by color alone (DESIGN.md 5.2, 13; CC-CAT-003)

warming.notes: Badge is always paired with the plain-language line (DESIGN.md 5.2, 13; CC-CAT-003)

cacheMiss.plainLine: Not available in your region yet
cacheMiss.notes: Badge is always paired with the plain-language line (DESIGN.md 5.2, 13; CC-CAT-003)

contrastPairs.name: char.900 on paper.100
contrastPairs.usage: All text; DESIGN.md 3.2 states this passes AA and AAA for all text, so the enforced floor is WCAG AAA normal text (7:1)

contrastPairs.name: char.900 on butcher.300
contrastPairs.usage: All text; DESIGN.md 3.2 states this passes AA and AAA for all text, so the enforced floor is WCAG AAA normal text (7:1)

contrastPairs.name: ember.500 on paper.100
contrastPairs.usage: Large text and graphics ONLY (WCAG AA large 3:1); body text is never ember.500 on Paper — use ember.700 (DESIGN.md 3.2)

contrastPairs.name: ember.700 on paper.100
contrastPairs.usage: Colored body-size text on light surfaces when 4.5:1 is required (WCAG AA normal text; DESIGN.md 3.2)

contrastPairs.name: cache.500 on pit.950
contrastPairs.usage: Dashboard text and badges (WCAG AA normal text; DESIGN.md 3.2)

contrastPairs.name: paper.100 on ember.500
contrastPairs.usage: Button label text on Ember fill; DESIGN.md 3.2 states it passes at button sizes (WCAG AA large 3:1)
